report_metadata.cancer_type: Recurrent/metastatic head and neck squamous cell carcinoma (R/M HNSCC)
report_metadata.stage: Stage IVC (recurrence with lung metastases, prior chemoRT for stage III oropharyngeal SCC)

treatments.name: Pembrolizumab Monotherapy (First-Line for CPS>=20)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 3 RCT KEYNOTE-048 with 882 patients and 5-year follow-up (69.2 months median). Established standard of care for R/M HNSCC with CPS>=20

survival_benefit.rationale: In CPS>=20: median OS 14.9 vs 10.7 months (HR 0.61, 95% CI 0.46-0.81). 5-year OS 14.4% vs 6.5% for EXTREME. More than doubles long-term survival. TMB-high may further enhance benefit

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved first-line for R/M HNSCC with PD-L1 CPS>=1. Widely available globally. No platinum required — avoids cisplatin toxicity concern for this patient with CKD stage 2 and hearing loss

safety_profile.rationale: Well tolerated compared to chemotherapy regimens. No nephrotoxicity or ototoxicity. Immune-related AEs manageable. Avoids platinum entirely, critical for this patient with CKD stage 2 and cisplatin-induced hearing loss

biomarker_match.rationale: CPS>=20 is the optimal biomarker subgroup with HR 0.61. TMB-high further supports immunotherapy benefit. Patient has both favorable biomarkers

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Pembrolizumab is a humanized anti-PD-1 monoclonal antibody that blocks the interaction between PD-1 and its ligands PD-L1/PD-L2, restoring anti-tumor T cell activity. In CPS>=20 tumors, the high PD-L1 expression indicates an inflamed tumor microenvironment highly responsive to checkpoint blockade.

treatments.name: Pembrolizumab + Carboplatin + 5-FU (First-Line Combination)

evidence_level.rationale: KEYNOTE-048 Phase 3 RCT demonstrated superiority of pembrolizumab+chemo vs EXTREME across all PD-L1 subgroups. Carboplatin substitution for cisplatin supported by NCCN guidelines

survival_benefit.rationale: In CPS>=20: HR 0.63 (95% CI 0.47-0.84). In total population: HR 0.72 (95% CI 0.60-0.86). 5-year OS 16.0% vs 5.2%. Broader activity than monotherapy but patient CPS>=20 favors monotherapy first

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved first-line for R/M HNSCC regardless of PD-L1 status. Carboplatin widely available as cisplatin substitute

safety_profile.rationale: Carboplatin less nephrotoxic and ototoxic than cisplatin but still carries myelosuppression risk. Patient has CKD stage 2 — carboplatin requires dose adjustment by GFR (Calvert formula). Adds chemotherapy toxicity vs pembrolizumab monotherapy

biomarker_match.rationale: Effective across all CPS levels. For CPS>=20, HR 0.63 is comparable to monotherapy HR 0.61. TMB-high supports immunotherapy component

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Combines anti-PD-1 checkpoint blockade (pembrolizumab) with platinum-based chemotherapy (carboplatin substituted for cisplatin due to CKD/ototoxicity) and antimetabolite (5-fluorouracil). Chemotherapy induces immunogenic cell death releasing tumor antigens while pembrolizumab unleashes anti-tumor T cell responses.
biomarker_requirements: None required — effective across all PD-L1 levels
GFR-based carboplatin dosing required for CKD
notable_side_effects: renal function monitoring required for carboplatin dosing

treatments.name: Nivolumab (Second-Line After Platinum/Immunotherapy)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 3 RCT CheckMate-141 (n=361) with 2-year follow-up. Established standard of care for second-line R/M HNSCC. Published in NEJM

survival_benefit.rationale: Median OS 7.5 vs 5.1 months (HR 0.68, 95% CI 0.54-0.86). 2-year OS 16.9% vs 6.0%. Tripled long-term survival. Benefit greater in PD-L1>=1% (HR 0.55)

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved for R/M HNSCC after platinum-based therapy. Widely available globally

safety_profile.rationale: Grade 3-4 AEs only 15.3% vs 36.9% for investigator choice. Well tolerated. No nephrotoxicity or ototoxicity — excellent for this patient

biomarker_match.rationale: Benefit seen in PD-L1>=1% (HR 0.55) and <1% (HR 0.73). This patient's CPS>=20 likely correlates with high tumor PD-L1 expression. TMB-high may further predict benefit

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Nivolumab is a fully human anti-PD-1 IgG4 monoclonal antibody that blocks PD-1/PD-L1 interaction, restoring T cell-mediated anti-tumor immunity. Distinct from pembrolizumab in binding epitope, offering potential benefit after pembrolizumab exposure.

treatments.name: Carboplatin + Cetuximab + 5-FU (Modified EXTREME)

evidence_level.rationale: EXTREME Phase 3 trial (n=442) established cetuximab + platinum + 5-FU as standard. Carboplatin substitution supported by NCCN for cisplatin-ineligible patients with multiple retrospective studies

survival_benefit.rationale: Original EXTREME: median OS 10.1 vs 7.4 months (HR 0.80). Carboplatin-modified EXTREME: median OS 7.2 months overall, 10.1 months in PS 0-1. Now superseded by pembrolizumab-based regimens in 1L

accessibility.rationale: All components FDA approved. Widely available globally. Standard NCCN-listed regimen

safety_profile.rationale: Carboplatin less nephrotoxic than cisplatin but still causes myelosuppression. Cetuximab causes skin rash (60-80%), infusion reactions. 5-FU causes mucositis, diarrhea. Overall significant toxicity burden

biomarker_match.rationale: EGFR universally overexpressed in HNSCC. No biomarker selection needed. However, now inferior to pembrolizumab-based regimens in PD-L1+ patients

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Cetuximab (anti-EGFR monoclonal antibody) blocks EGFR-mediated cell proliferation and survival. Combined with carboplatin (DNA crosslinking) and 5-fluorouracil (antimetabolite). Carboplatin substituted for cisplatin to reduce nephrotoxicity and ototoxicity in this patient.

key_evidence.journal: New England Journal of Medicine / Clinical Oncology

biomarker_requirements: None — EGFR universally expressed in HNSCC
treatments.availability: FDA Approved — NCCN listed alternative first-line for R/M HNSCC

treatments.name: Pembrolizumab + Cabozantinib

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 2 trial (n=36) showing impressive ORR 52%, median PFS 14.6 months, median OS 22.3 months. Not yet Phase 3 confirmed but strong signal

survival_benefit.rationale: Median OS 22.3 months (95% CI 11.7-32.9) and median PFS 14.6 months far exceed historical benchmarks. 2-year OS 54.7%. Clinical benefit rate 91%

accessibility.rationale: Both drugs FDA approved individually but combination not approved for HNSCC. Off-label use possible. Cabozantinib approved for RCC/HCC

safety_profile.rationale: Grade 3+ AEs 17%. Cabozantinib dose reduction needed in 44.4% of patients. Main AEs: fatigue (44%), diarrhea (33%), hypothyroidism (33%). No significant nephrotoxicity or ototoxicity

biomarker_match.rationale: Cabozantinib targets VEGFR/MET/AXL which may enhance immunotherapy response. TMB-high and CPS>=20 support immunotherapy backbone. No specific biomarker required for cabozantinib

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Cabozantinib is a multikinase TKI targeting VEGFR-1/2/3, MET, AXL, and TAM receptor kinases. Combined with pembrolizumab (anti-PD-1), it creates an immunomodulatory tumor microenvironment by reducing VEGF-mediated immunosuppression and targeting immune-evasion pathways while pembrolizumab restores T cell function.

key_evidence.study_name: Phase 2 Pembrolizumab + Cabozantinib in R/M HNSCC

biomarker_requirements: VEGFR/MET expression may predict cabozantinib benefit
treatments.availability: Off-label combination — both drugs individually FDA approved

treatments.name: Petosemtamab + Pembrolizumab (First-Line, Investigational)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 2 trial (n=43 evaluable) with robust data. Phase 3 LiGeR-HN1 trial actively recruiting (NCT06525220). Strong efficacy signal justifying rapid Phase 3 progression

survival_benefit.rationale: ORR 63% in 1L PD-L1+ R/M HNSCC. 12-month OS rate 79%. Median PFS 9 months. Substantial improvement over pembrolizumab monotherapy historical ORR of 23%

accessibility.rationale: Available through Phase 3 LiGeR-HN1 trial only. Actively recruiting globally as of September 2024. Not yet FDA approved

safety_profile.rationale: Manageable safety profile. Skin toxicity and infusion reactions are the main concerns. No unexpected signals

biomarker_match.rationale: Targets EGFR (universally expressed in HNSCC) and LGR5 (cancer stem cell marker). PD-L1+ population studied. Patient's CPS>=20 qualifies for trial entry

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Petosemtamab (MCLA-158) is a bispecific antibody targeting EGFR and LGR5 with enhanced ADCC activity. It simultaneously blocks EGFR signaling and eliminates LGR5+ cancer stem cells responsible for treatment resistance and tumor regeneration. Combined with pembrolizumab for immune checkpoint blockade.

key_evidence.study_name: Phase 2 Petosemtamab + Pembrolizumab in 1L R/M HNSCC

biomarker_requirements: LGR5 expression (not routinely tested; EGFR universal in HNSCC)
treatments.availability: Clinical Trial Only — Phase 3 LiGeR-HN1 recruiting (NCT06525220)

treatments.name: Ficerafusp Alfa + Pembrolizumab (First-Line, Investigational)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 1/1b expansion cohort (n=28 for HPV-negative CPS>=1). Impressive early data. FDA Breakthrough Therapy Designation. Phase 2/3 FORTIFI-HN01 enrolling ~650 patients

survival_benefit.rationale: Confirmed ORR 54% in HPV-negative CPS>=1 (21% CR). Median DOR 21.7 months. Median OS 21.3 months. Median PFS 9.9 months. Substantial improvement over pembrolizumab monotherapy

accessibility.rationale: Available only through clinical trial (FORTIFI-HN01 Phase 2/3). FDA BTD granted — potential for accelerated approval. Not yet commercially available

safety_profile.rationale: Manageable safety profile in Phase 1b. No unexpected toxicities beyond known class effects of anti-EGFR and anti-PD-1 therapies. Skin toxicity reported

biomarker_match.rationale: Studied in PD-L1 CPS>=1 population. Targets EGFR (universally expressed in HNSCC) plus TGF-beta trap. Patient's CPS>=20 and TMB-high may enhance benefit. Excludes HPV+ oropharynx in trial — patient had oropharyngeal primary

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Ficerafusp alfa (BCA101) is a bifunctional fusion protein combining anti-EGFR antibody activity with a TGF-beta trap. It simultaneously blocks EGFR-driven tumor growth and neutralizes TGF-beta-mediated immunosuppression in the tumor microenvironment. Combined with pembrolizumab for enhanced anti-tumor immunity.
treatments.availability: Clinical Trial Only — FDA Breakthrough Therapy Designation granted

treatments.name: Petosemtamab Monotherapy (Second-Line+)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 2 trial (n=75 evaluable) in 2L+ R/M HNSCC. Phase 3 LiGeR-HN2 trial actively recruiting (NCT06496178). Presented at ESMO Asia 2024

survival_benefit.rationale: ORR 36% in 2L+ setting. Median OS 11.4 months — substantially better than historical second-line options (methotrexate/docetaxel OS ~6 months)

accessibility.rationale: Available through Phase 3 LiGeR-HN2 trial. Actively recruiting since July 2024. Not yet approved

safety_profile.rationale: Tolerable safety profile. Skin toxicity manageable. No significant nephrotoxicity or ototoxicity

biomarker_match.rationale: Targets EGFR and LGR5 — both relevant in HNSCC. No specific biomarker required for eligibility

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Petosemtamab (MCLA-158) bispecific antibody with ADCC-enhanced activity targeting EGFR and LGR5. Eliminates LGR5+ cancer stem cells while blocking EGFR-driven proliferation, addressing treatment resistance mechanisms.

key_evidence.study_name: Phase 2 Petosemtamab Monotherapy in 2L+ R/M HNSCC

biomarker_requirements: None required — EGFR universally expressed in HNSCC
treatments.availability: Clinical Trial Only — Phase 3 LiGeR-HN2 recruiting (NCT06496178)

treatments.name: Alpelisib (PIK3CA-Mutated HNSCC)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 2 TRIUMPH trial (n=42 enrolled, 33 evaluable) — biomarker-driven umbrella trial. Published analysis of differential efficacy by PIK3CA mutation site (2025)

survival_benefit.rationale: ORR 21.2%. Median PFS 3.4 months. Median OS 12.4 months. Disease control rate 65.6%. Better for helical domain mutations (PFS 7.3 vs 1.6 months for H1047R)

accessibility.rationale: Alpelisib FDA approved for PIK3CA-mutated HR+/HER2- breast cancer. Off-label use possible in HNSCC. KURRENT-HN combination trials ongoing

safety_profile.rationale: Hyperglycemia grade 3+ in 45.2% — significant concern. Dose delays 57.1%, reductions 38.1%, discontinuation 23.8%. Requires glucose monitoring and potential metformin prophylaxis

biomarker_match.rationale: Patient has confirmed PIK3CA mutation — direct biomarker match. Alpelisib selectively inhibits PI3Kα. Efficacy depends on specific mutation site (helical vs kinase domain)

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Alpelisib is a selective PI3Kα inhibitor that blocks the phosphoinositide 3-kinase pathway activated by PIK3CA mutations. In PIK3CA-mutated HNSCC, it inhibits PI3K/AKT/mTOR signaling, reducing cell proliferation, survival, and resistance to apoptosis.
biomarker_requirements: Helical domain mutations (E542K, E545K) show better PFS than H1047R kinase domain mutations
treatments.availability: FDA Approved for PIK3CA-mutated breast cancer — off-label for HNSCC

treatments.name: Docetaxel Monotherapy (Second/Third-Line Chemotherapy)

evidence_level.rationale: Long-established standard second-line option per NCCN. Used as investigator's choice comparator in CheckMate-141, LiGeR-HN2, and other trials. Decades of clinical experience

survival_benefit.rationale: Median OS approximately 6 months as second-line monotherapy. ORR 7-13%. Limited durable benefit. Primarily palliative role after immunotherapy failure

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved, generic, inexpensive, available worldwide. Standard NCCN-listed option

safety_profile.rationale: Significant toxicity: neutropenia (grade 3+: 30-40%), mucositis, fatigue, neuropathy, alopecia. No nephrotoxicity or ototoxicity — acceptable for this patient

biomarker_match.rationale: No biomarker required — universal applicability. Activity independent of PD-L1, TMB, or PIK3CA status

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Docetaxel is a taxane that stabilizes microtubules, preventing cell division and inducing apoptosis. It disrupts mitotic spindle assembly in rapidly dividing cancer cells.

treatments.name: Methotrexate Monotherapy (Second/Third-Line)

evidence_level.rationale: Decades of clinical use. NCCN-listed standard second-line option. Used as investigator's choice comparator in multiple trials (CheckMate-141, LiGeR-HN2)

survival_benefit.rationale: Median OS approximately 5-6 months. ORR 5-10%. Primarily palliative. Inferior to immunotherapy and ADCs in modern era but remains an option after IO failure

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved, generic, very inexpensive, available globally. Weekly dosing is convenient

safety_profile.rationale: Generally better tolerated than taxanes. Mucositis, myelosuppression, hepatotoxicity, renal toxicity at high doses. Weekly 40 mg/m2 dosing is manageable. Requires renal dose adjustment for CKD stage 2

biomarker_match.rationale: No biomarker required — universal applicability. Antimetabolite activity independent of molecular profile

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Methotrexate is a folate antimetabolite that inhibits dihydrofolate reductase (DHFR), blocking thymidylate and purine synthesis required for DNA replication. Induces cell death in rapidly dividing cancer cells.

treatments.name: Enfortumab Vedotin (ADC, Second-Line+)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 2 EV-202 trial (n=46 evaluable) in heavily pretreated HNSCC. Published in JCO 2025. Single-arm but clear activity signal

survival_benefit.rationale: ORR 23.9% in heavily pretreated population. Clinically meaningful responses in treatment-refractory setting where options are very limited

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved for urothelial carcinoma. Off-label use in HNSCC possible. Nectin-4 expression in HNSCC supports biological rationale

safety_profile.rationale: Known MMAE-based ADC toxicities: peripheral neuropathy, skin reactions, hyperglycemia. Manageable but requires monitoring. No nephrotoxicity or ototoxicity concerns

biomarker_match.rationale: Targets Nectin-4, which is expressed in subset of HNSCC. No standard Nectin-4 testing in HNSCC. Universal applicability not established

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Enfortumab vedotin is an antibody-drug conjugate targeting Nectin-4 on tumor cells. It delivers the microtubule-disrupting agent MMAE directly to Nectin-4-expressing cancer cells, causing mitotic arrest and cell death. Nectin-4 is expressed in a subset of HNSCC tumors.
biomarker_requirements: Nectin-4 expression (not routinely tested in HNSCC)
treatments.availability: FDA Approved for urothelial cancer — off-label or trial for HNSCC

treatments.name: Tipifarnib (HRAS-Mutated HNSCC — Not This Patient)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 2 AIM-HN pivotal trial (n=50 evaluable). FDA Breakthrough Therapy Designation. Registration-directed study for potential accelerated approval

survival_benefit.rationale: ORR 29% in second-line HRAS-mutant HNSCC. First targeted therapy for this rare subset. Meaningful responses in treatment-refractory setting

accessibility.rationale: Not yet FDA approved. Breakthrough designation supports accelerated pathway. Available through clinical trials

safety_profile.rationale: Well tolerated — grade 3-4 AEs only 10%. Discontinuation due to AEs only 7%. Favorable safety profile compared to chemotherapy

biomarker_match.rationale: Requires HRAS mutation — this patient has PIK3CA mutation, NOT HRAS. Tipifarnib is not applicable unless HRAS co-mutation discovered. Included for completeness as HNSCC-specific targeted therapy

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Tipifarnib is a farnesyltransferase inhibitor that blocks post-translational modification of HRAS protein, preventing its membrane localization and oncogenic signaling. Selectively active in tumors with HRAS mutations or amplification.
biomarker_requirements: HRAS missense mutation (required — not present in this patient)
treatments.availability: Clinical Trial / FDA Breakthrough Therapy Designation — not yet approved

treatments.name: Tisotumab Vedotin (ADC, Second-Line+)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 1/2 data in R/M HNSCC showing ORR 16% and median OS 9.4 months. Tissue factor widely expressed in HNSCC. FDA approved for cervical cancer

survival_benefit.rationale: ORR 16% at 2 mg/kg, median PFS 4.2 months, median OS 9.4 months in pretreated R/M HNSCC. Modest but meaningful in refractory setting

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved for cervical cancer (2021). Off-label use for HNSCC possible. Ongoing combination trials with chemoradiotherapy

safety_profile.rationale: Key toxicities include ocular events (conjunctivitis, dry eye requiring prophylaxis), peripheral neuropathy, epistaxis. Manageable with dose modifications. No nephro/ototoxicity

biomarker_match.rationale: Targets tissue factor (TF/CD142) which is broadly expressed in HNSCC. No specific biomarker testing required for tissue factor

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Tisotumab vedotin is an ADC targeting tissue factor (TF/CD142), delivering MMAE cytotoxic payload to TF-expressing tumor cells. Tissue factor is overexpressed in many solid tumors including HNSCC. Additional mechanisms include ADCC, bystander killing, and immunogenic cell death.

key_evidence.study_name: Phase 1/2 Tisotumab Vedotin in R/M HNSCC

biomarker_requirements: Tissue factor expression (broadly expressed in HNSCC, no routine testing needed)
treatments.availability: FDA Approved for cervical cancer — off-label or trial for HNSCC

clinical_trials.title: LiGeR-HN1: Petosemtamab + Pembrolizumab vs Pembrolizumab in First-Line PD-L1+ R/M HNSCC
clinical_trials.early_results: Phase 2 data: ORR 63%, 12-month OS rate 79%, median PFS 9 months with petosemtamab + pembrolizumab in 1L PD-L1+ R/M HNSCC

clinical_trials.title: LiGeR-HN2: Petosemtamab Monotherapy vs Investigator Choice (Cetuximab, Methotrexate, or Docetaxel) in Previously Treated R/M HNSCC
clinical_trials.early_results: Phase 2 monotherapy data: ORR 36%, median OS 11.4 months in 2L+ R/M HNSCC

clinical_trials.title: Ficerafusp Alfa + Pembrolizumab vs Placebo + Pembrolizumab in 1L Recurrent/Metastatic HNSCC (HPV-Negative, PD-L1 CPS>=1)
sites: US (Memorial Sloan Kettering, other major centers)
clinical_trials.early_results: Phase 1b: confirmed ORR 54% (21% CR) in HPV-neg CPS>=1. Median DOR 21.7 months. Median OS 21.3 months. FDA Breakthrough Therapy Designation granted

clinical_trials.title: Domvanalimab + Zimberelimab + Chemotherapy vs Zimberelimab + Chemotherapy in Previously Untreated R/M HNSCC
sites: US (MD Anderson Cancer Center, other centers)
clinical_trials.early_results: Domvanalimab (anti-TIGIT) combined with zimberelimab (anti-PD-1) — dual checkpoint blockade approach. Early data pending

clinical_trials.title: Cabozantinib + Nivolumab in IO-Refractory HNSCC and Melanoma Stratified by Tumor Biomarkers
clinical_trials.early_results: Building on Phase 2 cabozantinib + pembrolizumab data (ORR 52%, median OS 22.3 months). Evaluating cabozantinib with nivolumab specifically in IO-refractory setting

clinical_trials.title: Tipifarnib + Alpelisib Combination in HRAS and PIK3CA Dysregulated HNSCC
biomarker_requirements: HRAS mutation or PIK3CA gain-of-function mutation
clinical_trials.early_results: QSP modeling identifies PIK3CA gain-of-function as genotype most likely to benefit from combination. Patient's PIK3CA mutation qualifies for this trial

clinical_trials.title: KEYNOTE-048: Pembrolizumab +/- Chemotherapy vs EXTREME in R/M HNSCC (Long-Term Follow-up)
biomarker_requirements: PD-L1 CPS tested (stratified by CPS>=20, >=1, total)
clinical_trials.early_results: 5-year follow-up (2025): Pembrolizumab mono in CPS>=20 HR 0.61, pembro+chemo total population HR 0.72. 5-year OS 14.4% vs 6.5% for EXTREME

combination_strategies.rationale: Cabozantinib targets VEGFR/MET/AXL, reducing immunosuppressive tumor microenvironment and enhancing T cell infiltration. VEGF inhibition reverses immune evasion. Synergizes with PD-1 blockade. Avoids platinum toxicity — ideal for this CKD/hearing loss patient

Ficerafusp alfa blocks EGFR proliferation signaling and traps TGF-beta immunosuppressive cytokine. TGF-beta neutralization reverses immune exclusion. Triple mechanism (anti-EGFR + TGF-beta trap + anti-PD-1) addresses multiple resistance mechanisms

Petosemtamab eliminates LGR5+ cancer stem cells while blocking EGFR signaling. Cancer stem cell depletion may prevent resistance and relapse. ADCC-enhanced bispecific antibody recruits immune cells. Combined with PD-1 blockade for comprehensive anti-tumor effect

Dual pathway inhibition of PI3K and RAS signaling. Patient's PIK3CA mutation makes this combination biologically rational. QSP modeling identifies PIK3CA gain-of-function as most responsive genotype. Addresses resistance mechanisms through complementary pathway blockade

combination_strategies.evidence_level: Phase 3 KEYNOTE-048 (HR 0.72 total population, HR 0.63 CPS>=20)
combination_strategies.rationale: Chemotherapy causes immunogenic cell death releasing neoantigens, synergizing with PD-1 blockade. Carboplatin substituted for cisplatin given patient's CKD stage 2 and hearing loss. Standard 1L option when rapid response needed or concern about pembrolizumab monotherapy PFS (shorter initial PFS vs chemo combo)

supportive_care.evidence: NCCN guidelines, KDIGO chronic kidney disease guidelines. CKD stage 2 (GFR 60-89 mL/min) requires careful drug dosing and avoidance of nephrotoxic agents
supportive_care.benefit: Preserves renal function enabling continued systemic therapy. Carboplatin dosed by Calvert formula (GFR-based). Avoids cisplatin which is absolutely contraindicated. Regular creatinine/GFR monitoring prevents acute kidney injury from immunotherapy-related nephritis

supportive_care.evidence: ASCO guidelines on cisplatin ototoxicity management. Patient has pre-existing cisplatin-induced hearing loss from prior chemoRT
supportive_care.benefit: Avoids further ototoxic agents. Hearing aid fitting if not already done. Baseline and serial audiometry monitoring during treatment. Ototoxicity from cisplatin is irreversible — further platinum exposure absolutely avoided

supportive_care.evidence: ESPEN guidelines on nutrition in cancer patients (2021). Head and neck cancer patients have 30-50% malnutrition rate. Protein target 1.2-1.5 g/kg/day
supportive_care.benefit: Prevents treatment delays from malnutrition. Maintains muscle mass and ECOG performance status. Speech-language pathology for swallowing assessment — especially important given prior oropharyngeal radiation. PEG tube consideration if oral intake insufficient

supportive_care.evidence: ASCO/NCCN guidelines on management of immune-related adverse events from checkpoint inhibitors (2024 update). Applies to pembrolizumab and nivolumab therapy
supportive_care.benefit: Early detection of pneumonitis, colitis, hepatitis, nephritis, thyroiditis, and endocrinopathies enables prompt corticosteroid intervention. Baseline thyroid function, liver function, and renal function testing. Monitoring schedule: every 2-3 weeks during induction, every 6 weeks during maintenance

supportive_care.evidence: ACSM exercise guidelines for cancer survivors (2019). Meta-analyses show exercise reduces cancer-related fatigue by 25-30% and improves overall survival in head and neck cancer
supportive_care.benefit: Maintains functional status and ECOG performance score. Reduces immunotherapy-related fatigue. Improves quality of life. Range-of-motion exercises for neck/shoulder to manage fibrosis from prior radiation. Resistance training to prevent sarcopenia

supportive_care.approach: Psychosocial Support and Mental Health Services
supportive_care.evidence: NCCN Distress Management guidelines. Head and neck cancer patients have highest rates of depression (40%) and anxiety among solid tumor patients due to cosmetic/functional impact
supportive_care.benefit: Reduces depression/anxiety rates. Improves treatment adherence. Cognitive behavioral therapy and support groups show measurable quality of life improvements. Young patient (45 y.o.) with recurrent disease faces significant psychological burden
